bonuses.leadin: A sheep sent to ask this deity to end death gets distracted by salted grass, and the dog sent afterwards accidentally asks him to have dead men stay dead instead. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A sheep sent to ask this deity to end death gets distracted by salted grass, and the dog sent afterwards accidentally asks him to have dead men stay dead instead. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this creator deity of the Akan people. This sky god requests the capture of creatures like the leopard Osebo and the fairy Mmoatia (“mm-mo-AH-tee-uh”) in exchange for the world’s stories.

Osebo and Mmoatia are captured by Aso and her husband, Anansi, who exemplifies this archetype. The African-American folk figure Br’er (“brair”) Rabbit is another example of this mischievous archetype.

Description acceptable. Anansi’s capture of Mmoatia is often cited as a precursor story to Br’er Rabbit being caught with this object. Br’er Rabbit gets stuck when he hits this object for not returning his greeting, but tricks Br’er Fox into throwing him into a thicket to escape.
parts_sanitized: Name this creator deity of the Akan people. This sky god requests the capture of creatures like the leopard Osebo and the fairy Mmoatia ("mm-mo-AH-tee-uh") in exchange for the world's stories.

Osebo and Mmoatia are captured by Aso and her husband, Anansi, who exemplifies this archetype. The African-American folk figure Br'er ("brair") Rabbit is another example of this mischievous archetype.

Description acceptable. Anansi's capture of Mmoatia is often cited as a precursor story to Br'er Rabbit being caught with this object. Br'er Rabbit gets stuck when he hits this object for not returning his greeting, but tricks Br'er Fox into throwing him into a thicket to escape.
answers: tar baby [accept answers indicating a sticky doll or baby that is made out of pine tar and turpentine; prompt on baby or doll]
answers_sanitized: Nyame [accept Nyankapon-Nyame-Odomankoma or Onyame or Nyankopon or Onyankopong or Odomankoma]
tricksters [or trickster spirits; or trickster gods; or trickster figures]
tar baby [accept answers indicating a sticky doll or baby that is made out of pine tar and turpentine; prompt on baby or doll]

bonuses.leadin: Many African cultures have shared stories about this trickster god. For 10 points each:
parts: Nyame agrees to give this god all of the stories in the world as long as he captured several creatures such as Onini the python that were plaguing the people.
Anansi often appeared as a human but is best known for taking this form.
Anansi once tangled with this creation in a story that was a forerunner to a similar story featuring Brer Rabbit and Brer Fox.
